metadata.title: Switch
metadata.description: The switch component provides a clear way for users to toggle options or settings on and off. Use switch for binary controls that take effect immediately, like enabling features, activating settings, or controlling visibility.

properties.description: Controls whether the label is visible to all users or only to screen readers. visible: The label is shown to everyone (default).exclusive: The label is visually hidden but still announced by screen readers. Use exclusive when the surrounding context makes the label redundant visually, but screen reader users still need it for clarity.
Whether the control is currently checked. Use this for controlled components where you manage the checked state.
The value used in form data when the checkbox is checked.
The initial checked state for uncontrolled components. Use this when you want the control to start checked but don't need to control its state afterward.

A label that describes the purpose or content of the component for assistive technologies like screen readers. Use this to provide additional context when the visible content alone doesn't clearly convey the component's purpose.

Supplementary text displayed below the checkbox to provide additional context, instructions, or help. Use this to explain what checking the box means or provide guidance to users. This text is announced to screen readers.
An error message displayed below the checkbox to indicate validation problems. When set, the checkbox is styled with error indicators and the message is announced to screen readers.
The text label displayed next to the checkbox that describes what the checkbox controls. Clicking the label will also toggle the checkbox state.

Whether the field needs a value. This requirement adds semantic value to the field, but it will not cause an error to appear automatically. If you want to present an error when this field is empty, you can do so with the error property.
Whether the field is disabled, preventing any user interaction.
A unique identifier for the element. Use this to reference the element in JavaScript, link labels to form controls, or target specific elements for styling or scripting.
The name attribute for the field, used to identify the field's value when the form is submitted. Must be unique within the nearest containing form.

examples.name: Main example
examples.html_code:
<s-switch
  label="Enable feature"
  details="Ensure all criteria are met before enabling"
></s-switch>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.switch :enable_feature, label: "Enable feature", details: "Ensure all criteria are met before enabling" %>

examples.name: Toggle a setting
examples.description: Give users a clear way to turn a feature on or off. This example pairs a label with a toggle switch.
examples.html_code:
<s-switch
  label="Enable feature"
  details="Ensure all criteria are met before enabling"
></s-switch>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.switch :enable_feature, label: "Enable feature", details: "Ensure all criteria are met before enabling" %>

examples.name: Show a disabled switch
examples.description: Indicate when a feature isn't available. This example locks a switch to prevent interaction while displaying its current state.
examples.html_code:
<s-switch
  id="disabled-switch"
  label="Feature locked (Premium plan required)"
  checked
  disabled
></s-switch>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.switch :feature_locked_premium_plan_required, id: "disabled-switch", label: "Feature locked (Premium plan required)", checked: true, disabled: true %>

examples.name: Submit multiple settings in a form
examples.description: Collect multiple settings that save together. This example groups switches in a form for batch submission.
examples.html_code:
<form>
  <s-stack gap="base">
    <s-switch
      id="email-notifications"
      label="Email notifications"
      name="emailNotifications"
      value="enabled"
    ></s-switch>
    <s-switch
      id="sms-notifications"
      label="SMS notifications"
      name="smsNotifications"
      value="enabled"
    ></s-switch>
    <s-button type="submit">Save preferences</s-button>
  </s-stack>
</form>
examples.erb_code:
<form>
  <s-stack gap="base">
    <%= form.switch :email_notifications, id: "email-notifications", label: "Email notifications", value: "enabled" %>
    <%= form.switch :sms_notifications, id: "sms-notifications", label: "SMS notifications", value: "enabled" %>
    <s-button type="submit">Save preferences</s-button>
  </s-stack>
</form>

examples.name: Apply multiple settings immediately
examples.description: Organize settings in a panel layout. This example arranges switches in a stack to display related preferences together.
examples.html_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <s-switch id="notifications-setting" label="Push notifications"></s-switch>
  <s-switch id="autosave-setting" label="Auto-save drafts"></s-switch>
  <s-switch
    id="analytics-setting"
    label="Usage analytics"
    checked
  ></s-switch>
</s-stack>
examples.erb_code:
<s-stack gap="base">
  <%= form.switch :push_notifications, id: "notifications-setting", label: "Push notifications" %>
  <%= form.switch :auto_save_drafts, id: "autosave-setting", label: "Auto-save drafts" %>
  <%= form.switch :usage_analytics, id: "analytics-setting", label: "Usage analytics", checked: true %>
</s-stack>

examples.name: Hide the label visually
examples.description: Keep switches accessible when labels aren't visually needed. This example uses a visually hidden label that screen readers can still announce.
examples.erb_code: <%= form.switch :toggle_feature, id: "hidden-label-switch", labelaccessibilityvisibility: "exclusive", label: "Toggle feature", checked: true %>

examples.name: Show validation errors
examples.description: Communicate switch-related problems clearly. This example displays helper text with an error message when a required switch isn't enabled.
examples.html_code:
<s-switch
  id="terms-switch"
  label="Agree to terms and conditions"
  details="You must agree to continue with the purchase"
  error="Agreement is required"
  name="termsAgreement"
  required
  value="agreed"
></s-switch>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.switch :terms_agreement, id: "terms-switch", label: "Agree to terms and conditions", details: "You must agree to continue with the purchase", error: "Agreement is required", required: true, value: "agreed" %>

examples.name: Add an accessibility label
examples.description: Provide extra context for screen reader users. This example adds an accessibility label that gives more detail than the visible label alone.
examples.html_code:
<s-switch
  id="event-switch"
  label="Feature toggle"
  accessibilityLabel="Toggle feature on or off"
></s-switch>
examples.erb_code: <%= form.switch :feature_toggle, id: "event-switch", label: "Feature toggle", accessibilitylabel: "Toggle feature on or off" %>
